details.calculation: ($285,937 / 21 total beneficiaries) = $13,616.05 per outcome. | A $1,000 donation achieves ≈ 0.0734 outcomes.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'The upkeep of our stable and dedicated care of our horses-the most crucial component of our equine-assisted programmes-can be very costly, especially because many of our horses are rescued or retired and are more advanced in age. Such care includes vaccinations, comprehensive veterinary and dental care, as well as customised dietary and nutritional provisions for each horse.'
details.formula: Highest level of evidence cited in impact claims

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: 16 therapy horses, many rescued or retired, receive dedicated care including vaccinations, veterinary, dental, and nutritional provisions.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without EQUAL, many rescued or retired horses would face uncertain futures, neglect, or inadequate care.

metrics.evidence_quote: The upkeep of our stable and dedicated care of our horses-the most crucial component of our equine-assisted programmes-can be very costly, especially because many of our horses are rescued or retired and are more advanced in age. Such care includes vaccinations, comprehensive veterinary and dental care, as well as customised dietary and nutritional provisions for each horse.
metrics.metric_name: Therapy Horses Provided Care and Welfare

metrics.context_qualifier: 5 therapy dogs, mentioned as rescued, are integrated into animal-assisted learning programs and receive care.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without EQUAL, these rescued dogs would face uncertain futures, neglect, or inadequate care.

metrics.evidence_quote: Join us in raising funds for EQUAL, a non-profit organisation that strengthens the emotional and mental well-being of people in need, using therapy horses and cats that have themselves been rescued and given second shot at life.
metrics.metric_name: Therapy Dogs Provided Care and Welfare

significant_events.source_quote: EQUAL launched an Equine Sponsorship campaign in January 2023, through which members of public and supporters of EQUAL could donate towards the care of our herd of 16 therapy horses.
significant_events.summary: Launched in January 2023 to raise funds for the care of EQUAL's 16 therapy horses, many of whom are rescued or retired.

significant_events.source_quote: EQUAL's Canine Programme Centre at Enabling Village was officially launched on 4 October 2022 by Mr Tan Kiat How, Senior Minister of State for National Development and Communications & Information.
significant_events.summary: Officially launched on 4 October 2022 at Enabling Village, expanding EQUAL's animal-assisted services to include canine-assisted learning.

significant_events.event_name: Formalised Animal Welfare Training Series for Volunteers
significant_events.source_quote: In 2022, EQUAL implemented its first formalised Animal Welfare Training series, equipping volunteers with essential knowledge and fundamental skills on animal husbandry for horses.
significant_events.summary: Implemented in 2022, equipping volunteers with essential knowledge and skills on animal husbandry for horses, including safety, stable work, and equine care.

significant_events.event_name: Canine-Assisted Learning Pilot Programme for Stroke Survivors
significant_events.source_quote: In collaboration with Stroke Support Station (S3) in March 2023, EQUAL piloted a three-part canine-assisted programme with our canine partner Teddy.
significant_events.summary: Piloted in March 2023 with Stroke Support Station (S3), a three-part program using canine partner Teddy to assist stroke survivors.

data.original_text: Founded in 2015, EQUAL (an IPC charity) strives to transform lives through Animal-Assisted Services. EQUAL partners with animals to provide experiential and educational programmes that help empower vulnerable persons such as children, youths, persons with special needs, families, and seniors by enhancing their social and emotional well-being and supporting social integration in a safe environment — building resilience and inclusive communities.
We empower vulnerable persons by enhancing their social and emotional well-being and social integration in partnership with animals and communities.
Each year, we proudly serve hundreds of clients from various institutions, including schools, children's homes, nursing homes, and other social service organisations.
EQUAL serves 1,500 beneficiaries every year, comprising youths, people with special needs, families, and the elderly with dementia and depression.

Join us in raising funds for EQUAL, a non-profit organisation that strengthens the emotional and mental well-being of people in need, using therapy horses and cats that have themselves been rescued and given second shot at life.

Through an Animal-assisted learning curriculum, they help people facing challenges open up to create changes in mindsets and behaviours that are deep and long-lasting; learning social emotional skill sets that are important but conventionally hard to teach.

Our programmes serve 3 groups of clients. 1. Children & Youth - By strengthening their emotional resilience, we help children and youth, including those with special needs, do better in school. 2. Families - By strengthening their emotional resilience, we help families with special needs members cope better with the challenges they face. 3. Seniors - By strengthening their emotional resilience, we improve the quality of life for seniors struggling with dementia and depression.

The use of animals such as horses is a powerful way to heal people via non-clinical means. A 2013 study by the Centre for Quantitative Medicine, DUKE-NUS GMS showed an improvement of 68.7% average in 157 Northlight School students in their Habits of Mind after participating in EQUAL's Equine-assisted Learning programmes; producing significant and rapid changes of social and emotional learning in the youths.

EQUAL has recently expanded to Canine Assisted Learning so we can reach out to more people in need and serve more Through EQUAL's animal-assisted services, our beneficiaries have experienced transformation and breakthroughs in emotional health, behaviours and relationships.
